records.chapter: Stubbornness undermines counsel
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 179. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Stubbornness destroys (good) advice.
records.arabicText: ١٧٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): اللَّجَاجَةُ تَسُلُّ الرَّأْيَ.

records.chapter: Greed as perpetual enslavement
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 180. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him said: Greed is a lasting slavery.
records.arabicText: ١٨٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الطَّمَعُ رِقٌّ مُؤَبَّدٌ.

records.chapter: Silence in wisdom versus speech in folly
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 182. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: There is no advantage ill keeping quiet about an issue of wisdom, just as there is no good in speaking out an unintelligent thing.
records.arabicText: ١٨٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا خَيْرَ فِي الصَّمْتِ عَنِ الْحُكْمِ، كَمَا أَنَّهُ لَا خَيْرَ فِي الْقوْلِ بِالْجَهْلِ.

records.chapter: Two calls signify one leads to misguidance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 183. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If there are two different calls then one (of them) must be towards misguidance.
records.arabicText: ١٨٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا اخْتَلَفَتْ دَعْوَتَانِ إِلَّا كَانَتْ إِحْدَاهُمَا ضَلاَلَةً.

records.chapter: Unwavering certainty in recognizing truth
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 184. Amir al-mu'minin, peace be upon him, said: I have never entertained doubt about right since I was shown it.
records.arabicText: ١٨٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا شَكَكْتُ فِي الْحَقِّ مُذْ أُرِيتُهُ.

records.chapter: Oppression leads to inevitable regret
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 186. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: He who takes the lead in oppression has to bite his hand (in repentance) tomorrow.
records.arabicText: ١٨٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لِلظَّالِمِ الْبَادِي غَداً بِكَفِّهِ عَضَّةٌ.

records.chapter: Imminent departure from this world
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 187. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The departure (from this world) is imminent.
records.arabicText: ١٨٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الرَّحِيلُ وَشِيكٌ.

records.chapter: Patience as relief versus impatience as destruction
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 189. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If patience does not give relief to a man impatience kills him.
records.arabicText: ١٨٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ لَمْ يُنْجِهِ الصَّبْرُ أَهْلَكَهُ الْجَزَعُ.

records.chapter: Human mortality and life's fleeting nature
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 191. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: In this world man is the target towards which the arrows of death fly, and is like that wealth whose destruction is quickened by hard ships. (In this world) with every drink there is suffocation and with every morsel there is choking. Here no one gets anything unless he loses something else, and not a day of his age advances till a day passes out from his life. Thus, we are helpers of death and our lives are the targets of mortality. How then can we expect everlasting life since the night and day do not raise anything high without quickly arranging for the destruction of whatever they have built and for the splitting asunder of whatever they have joined together.
records.arabicText: ١٩١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّمَا الْمَرْءُ فِى الدُّنْيَا غَرَضٌ تَنْتَضِلُ فِيهِ الْمَنَايَا وَنَهْبٌ تُبَادِرُهُ الْمَصَائِبُ وَمَعَ كُلِّ جُرْعَةٍ شَرَقٌ وَفِى كُلِّ أَكْلَةٍ غَصَصٌ وَلَا يَنَالُ الْعَبْدُ نِعْمَةً إِلَّا بِفِرَاقِ أُخْرَى وَلَا يَسْتَقْبِلُ يَوْماً مِنْ عُمُرِهِ إِلَّا بِفِرَاقِ آخَرَ مِنْ أَجَلِهِ فَنَحْنُ أَعْوَانُ الْمَنُونِ وَأَنْفُسُنَا نَصْبُ الْحُتُوفِ فَمِنْ أَيْنَ نَرْجُو الْبَقَاءَ وَهَذَا اللَّيْلُ وَالنَّهَارُ لَمْ يَرْفَعَا مِنْ شَيْ‏ءٍ شَرَفاً إِلَّا أَسْرَعَا الْكَرَّةَ فِى هَدْمِ مَا بَنَيَا وَ تَفْرِيقِ مَا جَمَعَا.

records.chapter: Wealth as stewardship beyond basic needs
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 192. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: O son of Adam, whatever you earn beyond your basic needs you are only a treasurer of it for others.
records.arabicText: ١٩٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): يَا ابْنَ آدَمَ مَا كَسَبْتَ فَوْقَ قُوتِكَ، فَأَنْتَ فِيهِ خَازِنٌ لِغَيْرِكَ.

records.chapter: Hearts act best when passionate and willing
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 193. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Hearts are imbued with passion and the power of advancing and retreating. Therefore, approach them for action at the time of their passionateness and when they are in a mood for advancing, because if hearts are forced (to do a thing) they will be blinded.
records.arabicText: ١٩٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ لِلْقُلُوبِ شَهْوَةً وَإِقْبَالًا وَإِدْبَاراً، فَأْتُوهَا مِنْ قِبَلِ شَهْوَتِهَا وَإِقْبَالِهَا، فَإِنَّ الْقَلْبَ إِذَا أُكْرِهَ عَمِيَ.

records.chapter: Misers' niggardly disputes over worthless possessions
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 195. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, passed beside a dump of rubbish full of filth and remarked: This is what the misers used to be niggardly about.
records.arabicText: ١٩٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) وَقَدْ مَرَّ بِقَذَرٍ عَلَى مَزْبَلَةٍ: هَذَا مَا بَخِلَ بِهِ الْبَاخِلُونَ.

records.chapter: Misers' niggardly disputes over worthless possessions
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: In another tradition it is related that he said: This is what you used to dispute with each other about until yesterday!
records.arabicText: وَرُوِيَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: هَذَا مَا كُنْتُمْ تَتَنَافَسُونَ فِيهِ بِالْأَمْسِ!

records.chapter: Hearts tire like bodies; seek refreshing wisdom
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 197. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The hearts become tired as the bodies become tired. You should therefore search for beautiful sayings for them (to enjoy by way of refreshment).
records.arabicText: ١٩٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ هذِهِ الْقُلُوبَ تَمَلُّ كَمَا تَمَلُّ الْأَبْدَانُ، فَابْتَغُوا لَهَا طَرَائِفَ الْحِكْمَةِ.

records.chapter: Khārijites' misinterpretation of divine verdict
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 198. When Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, heard the slogan of the Khārijites: There is no verdict save of Allāh, he said: This sentence is true but it is interpreted wrongly.
records.arabicText: ١٩٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لَمَّا سَمِعَ قَوْلَ الْخَوَارِجِ ـ لَا حُكْمَ إِلَّا لِلهِ ـ : كَلِمَةُ حَقٍّ يُرَادُ بِهَا بَاطِلٌ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn rebukes the crowd for appearing only in disgraceful situations
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 200. An offender was brought before Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, and there was a crowd of people with the man, so Amīr al-mu’minīn remarked: Woe to the faces who are seen only on foul occasions.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) وقد أُتي بِجَانٍ وَمَعَهُ غَوْغَاءُ: فَقَالَ : لَا مَرْحَباً بِوُجُوهٍ لَا تُرَى إِلَّا عِنْدَ كُلِّ سَوْأَةٍ .

records.chapter: Talḥah and Zubayr's conditional allegiance rejected by ʿAlī
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 202. When Ṭalḥah and az-Zubayr said to him: We are prepared to swear allegiance to you on condition that we have a share with you in this matter (of caliphate), Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: No, but you will have a share in strengthening (the caliphate) and in affording assistance and you will both be helping me at the time of need and hardship.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) وَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُ طَلْحَةُ وَالزُّبَيْرُ: نُبَايِعُكَ عَلَى أَنَّا شُرَكَاؤُكَ فِي هَذَا الْأَمْرِ: لَا، وَلكِنَّكُمَا شَرِيكَانِ فِي الْقُّوَّةَ وَالِاسْتِعَانَةِ، وَعَوْنَانِ عَلَى الْعَجْزِ وَالْأَوَدِ.

records.chapter: Knowledge expands unlike physical containers
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 205. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Every container gets narrower according to what is placed in it except the container of knowledge which expands instead.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): كُلُّ وِعَاءٍ يَضِيقُ بِمَا جُعِلَ فِيهِ إِلَّا وِعَاءَ الْعِلْمِ، فَإِنَّهُ يَتَّسِعُ بِهِ.

records.chapter: Forbearance earns allies against ignorance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 206. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The first reward the exerciser of forbearance gets is that people become his helpers against the ignorant.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أَوَّلُ عِوَضِ الْحَلِيمِ مِنْ حِلْمِهِ أَنَّ النَّاسَ أَنْصَارُهُ عَلَى الْجَاهِلِ.

records.chapter: Self-reflection benefits, neglect harms; fear ensures safety, observation yields wisdom
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 208. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever takes account of his self is benefited, and whoever remains neglectful of it suffers. Whoever fears remains safe; whoever takes instruction (from things around) perceives; and whoever perceives gains understanding, and whoever gains understanding secures knowledge.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ حَاسَبَ نَفْسَهُ رَبِحَ، وَمَنْ غَفَلَ عَنْهَا خَسِرَ، وَمَنْ خَافَ أَمِنَ، وَمَنِ اعْتَبَرَ أَبْصَرَ وَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ فَهِمَ وَمَنْ فَهِمَ عَلِمَ.

records.chapter: Fear Allah with preparation and urgency
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 210. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Fear Allāh like the one who prepares himself after extracting himself (from worldly affairs) and after getting ready in this way makes effort; then he acts quickly during the period of this life, hastens in view of the dangers (of falling into error) and has his eye on proceeding towards the goal, on the end of his journey and on the place of his (eventual) return.
records.arabicText: ٢١٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ تَقِيَّةَ مَنْ شَمَّرَ تَجْرِيداً وَجَدَّ تَشْمِيراً وَكَمَّشَ فِى مَهَلٍ وَبَادَرَ عَنْ وَجَلٍ وَنَظَرَ فِى كَرَّةِ الْمَوْئِلِ وَعَاقِبَةِ الْمَصْدَرِ وَمَغَبَّةِ الْمَرْجِعِ.

records.chapter: Virtues and vices in leadership and conduct
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 211. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Generosity is the protector of honour; forbearance is the bridle of the fool; forgiveness is the levy of success; disregard is the punishment of him who betrays; and consultation is the chief way of guidance. He who is content with his own opinion faces danger. Endurance braves calamities while impatience is a helper of the hardships of the world. The best contentment is to give up desires. Many a slavish mind is subservient to overpowering longings. Capability helps preservation of experience. Love means well-utilized relationship. Do not trust one who is grieved.
records.arabicText: ٢١١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْجُودُ حَارِسُ الْأَعْرَاضِ وَالْحِلْمُ فِدَامُ السَّفِيهِ وَالْعَفْوُ زَكَاةُ الظَّفَرِ وَالسُّلُوُّ عِوَضُكَ مِمَّنْ غَدَرَ وَالِاسْتِشَارَةُ عَيْنُ الْهِدَايَةِ وَقَدْ خَاطَرَ مَنِ اسْتَغْنَى بِرَأْيِهِ وَالصَّبْرُ يُنَاضِلُ الْحِدْثَانَ وَالْجَزَعُ مِنْ أَعْوَانِ الزَّمَانِ وَأَشْرَفُ الْغِنَى تَرْكُ الْمُنَى وَكَمْ مِنْ عَقْلٍ أَسِيرٍ تَحْتَ هَوَى أَمِيرٍ وَمِنَ التَّوْفِيقِ حِفْظُ التَّجْرِبَةِ وَالْمَوَدَّةُ قَرَابَةٌ مُسْتَفَادَةٌ وَلَا تَأْمَنَنَّ مَلُولًا.

records.chapter: Ignoring pain for lasting happiness
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 213. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Ignore pain otherwise you will never be happy. [Or according to another reading): Ignore pain and grief; you will ever be happy.] [1]
records.arabicText: ٢١٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أَغْضِ عَلَى الْقَذَى وَالْأَلَمِ تَرْضَ أَبَداً.

records.chapter: Soft-trunked trees bear thick branches
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 214. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The tree whose trunk is soft has thick branches. [1]
records.arabicText: ٢١٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ لَانَ عُودُهُ كَثُفَتْ أَغْصَانُهُ.

records.chapter: Generosity leads to honor or corruption
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 216. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: He who gives generously achieves position. (Or according to another interpretation): He who achieves position begins to make wrong use of it.
records.arabicText: ٢١٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ نَالَ اسْتَطَالَ.
